observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to verify their records relative to the status of the foregoing outstanding checks, after which, consider the following journal entry:
Debit            CIB-LCCA					P456,219.94
Credit		Prior Period Adjustment		P456,219.94
# To record reversion of stale checks issued in 2023
and previous years to the CIB
Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to record the other reconciling items presented in Annex A attached to AOM No. 2025-009 dated April 8, 2025 which was received by Management on April 22, 2025 and furnish a copy of the JEV to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) so that they will have a basis for the recording of adjustments in the cashbooks;
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to record the reconciling items in his cashbook based on the JEV provided by the Accounting Office to reflect the correct balances;
Instruct both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records regularly to immediately effect any adjustments affecting both records to show accurate and reliable balances; and
Instruct both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refrain from recording the undeposited collections at the end of the year as DIT in the books to present fairly the actual results of operations/transactions in their corresponding records, hence the FS show the correct and actual balances of the cash accounts, hence to present a more reliable information to users.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the MSWDO and the OIC-MAO to submit immediately to the Municipal Accountant the duly accomplished/certified distribution list of the issued/distributed food supplies amounting to P147,750.00 and farm implements and equipment aggregating P106,432,590.00 to the end-users respectively, for verification and as her basis in recording the items under the expense account, thus the affected accounts will be correctly stated in the FS.
Henceforth, the Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the GSO to regularly prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant the SSMI, supported with a duly accomplished RIS and duly accomplished and certified distribution list of all goods distributed to the end-users, as her basis in the preparation of journal entry to record the expenditures using appropriate expenditure accounts, hence to present fairly the FS.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to send demand letters and ensure that the same is received by the concerned persons and seek legal remedies so that the unliquidated cash advances of those accoutable persons who are no longer in service will be settled the soonest possible time. For the unliquidated cash advances of the deceased individuals, we recommended that the LCE seek legal remedies for the institution of claim against their estates. Thus, the Municipality could collect more funds that could finance projects for the benefit of its constituents.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts the fair market value of the six parcels of land to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the FS.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE:
instruct the Municipal Accountant to go over the foregoing transactions and prepare the necessary adjusting entries, considering the hereunder proposed journal entries:
require the Municipal Accountant that henceforth be guided by the IPSAS Chart of Accounts in recording financial transactions to reflect the correct balances of the accounts in the FS.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the necessary documents detailing the information relative to replaced parts of road networks and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant to serve as her basis in determining the appropriate carrying amounts of replaced parts of the assets to be derecognized from the books in order to fairly and reliably present the road networks account in the FS.

The Audit Team recommended that Management should be guided by the approved Annual Budget in its procurement activities ensuring the sufficiency or availability of appropriations before entering into contract in adherence to the provisions of Section 305 (a) of RA No. 7160 and Section 7.1 of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 to avoid budget deficit or entering into contract with no valid appropriation, hence to ascertain the validity and propriety of transactions and to ensure faithful performance of obligation by the LGU to the contracting parties.
Also, the Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to disclose the unpaid obligation amounting to P2,368,745.00 in the NFS in the ensuing year to present fairly the FS of the LGU.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting/correcting entry to reclassify the Due to LGUs totaling P1,816,621.45 to other income by drawing the hereunder journal entries:
CY 2025 Books of the General Fund
Henceforth, the Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to continually abide with the provisions of Budget Circular No. 004-5A dated October 7, 2005 in the utilization and disbursement of the proceeds from the sale of bidding documents and the treatment of the residual/unutilized balance, ensuring that the same shall form part of the local government funds, thus could be used for the implementation of other PPAs beneficial to the LGU’s constituents.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to make a compilation of any record/documentation and prepare the corresponding assessment/evaluation report as regards the long outstanding liabilities to support the possible reversion in order not to overstate the liability accounts.  Likewise, attach Subsidiary ledgers and all available records to support the amount of the JEV and attach a copy in case of reversion to the unappropriated surplus. If there may be claims in the future, the JEV would serve as the basis in requesting for a supplemental budget for the settlement of the payable.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the variance of P805,934.43 with the records of the Provincial Accountant to ensure correct and accurate balance of the Due to LGUs account in the FS and to ensure the correctness of the remittances made, thus FS will be fairly presented.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to trace the discrepancy between the balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances in the FS.

The Audit Team recommended that Management meticulously plan the LGU’s Budget ensuring the proper application of the budgetary requirements in adherence to relevant budgetary laws and guidelines and to ensure that the EME shall not exceed two percent (2%) of the actual receipts derived from basic real property tax in the next preceding calendar year, hence to ascertain that all disbursements/expenditures of the LGU are supported with valid appropriation.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the duly certified JEV together with the supporting documents and explanations of the transactions recorded in the GJ for CY 2024 and henceforth, to continually support the GJ with the same documentation to ascertain the validity and propriety of the financial transactions recorded therein and the correctness of journal entries made.
Further, the Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to follow the prescribed procedures in the recording of financial transactions from the JEV to the corresponding journals and then to the GL as embodied in Annexes 1 to 5 of Volume II of the MNGAS, ensuring that the necessary details, explanations, computations and supporting evidence/s are incorporated therein, hence to facilitate systematic verification of accounts presented in the FS.
Moreover, provide the names of the creditors and the date of incurrence of the obligation in the Schedule of Payables to facilitate verification of the same. Hence propriety and validity of the recorded liabilities could be easily evaluated.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to:
conform with the proper budgeting procedures and the process in the utilization of savings from completed PPAs as set forth in Sections 305 and 336 of RA No. 7160 and the Budget Operations Manual for LGUs;
ensure that savings from the completed PPAs funded under RA No. 7171 fund shall be appropriated and implemented for the authorized purposes or uses of the fund; and
ascertain that the budget is properly utilized and implemented, consistent with the planned programs, purposes and uses.
Hence, to avoid misappropriation or misuse of municipal funds and to prevent possible issuance of Notice of Disallowance and to ensure that the benefits from the proper implementation of PPAs will be duly delivered to the intended beneficiaries and constituents of the LGU.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Assessor to oversee the immediate transfer of the TCT and TD of the parcel of lands acquired in CYs 2023 and 2024 aggregating P41,677,440.00 in the name of the Municipality of Magsingal and to submit immediately the certified true copies of the TCT and TD of the parcels of land acquired in previous years aggregating P22,631,865.60 to ascertain absolute ownership over these parcels of land and to avoid possible ownership dispute which potentially will result to wastage of government funds.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and the officials concerned in the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travel and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to verify and locate the discrepancies of 353.11 liters, otherwise, refund the amount of P15,340.05.
Henceforth, the Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the concerned official to ensure that all consumed fuel is accurately reported in the Monthly Report of Official Travel and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and the Municipal Accountant to verify the completeness and correctness of the payments to avoid overpayment of undelivered goods, hence to safeguard government funds from wastage.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and other concerned officials to strictly observe and follow the provisions of Item 9.1 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2022-1 in the acquisition of motor vehicles and ensure that Management procure only brand-new item, thus, to ascertain its efficiency and future benefits to the LGU and to avoid the incurrence of loss or wastage of government funds. Henceforth, to support all acquisitions of motor vehicles with APMV in adherence to Item 7.1 of the same Circular, hence to ascertain the validity and propriety of the transactions.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE:
Instruct the concerned officials to submit immediately the lacking documents to the Municipal Accountant and require her to verify the completeness and accuracy thereof and subsequently submit the same to the Auditor in order for the latter to verify the validity, propriety and correctness of these documents and compliance of said transactions with the abovementioned provisions of law and regulations, non-compliance of which will result in the issuance of Notice of Suspension in accordance with COA Circular No. 2021-013 dated November 10, 2021.
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to demand from Elmer Salvador the refund of overpayment of his terminal leave benefits amounting to P11,000.00, otherwise, demand from the concerned official who overlooked the processing of the payment thereof;
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to demand from the former Punong Barangay Ferdinand Tapuro the refund of the cost of replaced waiting shed in the amount of P48,385.00 which was erroneously paid to him/credited to his account instead of the account of Barangay Dacutan; and
Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant and concerned officials of strict compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and COA Circular 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023 by supporting all disbursement vouchers with complete and accurate documents before effecting payment thereof to ascertain the validity, propriety and correctness of the transactions.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately the required documents and address the deficiencies noted as embodied in the TER to facilitate immediate inspection of the said infrastructure projects and to ascertain completeness, conformity with the provisions of the contract and proper implementation of the same and likewise to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension on the 28 infrastructure projects aggregating P463,636,182.34.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary documents for the application of bond for the GSO and pay for the corresponding bond premiums, hence to protect the interest of the government.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE:
instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare the disbursement vouchers for the remittance of the shares/dues to the barangays in the total amount of P1,439,354.49; and
henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to release the share of each barangay on RPT and CTCs on a quarterly basis within five days after the end of each quarter to enable the latter to utilize the funds for its operations.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort to trace the source agencies and immediately return the unutilized balances or to the Bureau of Treasury to finally end any obligation created by these transfers and to fully liquidate these accounts.
Otherwise, the Audit Team further recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to make a compilation of any record/documentation and assessment/evaluation report on the status of accomplishments of the foregoing projects/programs/activities to support the possible reversion to the general fund.  Attach subsidiary ledgers and all available records to support the amount of the JEV in case of reversion to the unappropriated surplus. If there may be claims in the future, the JEV would serve as the basis in requesting for a supplemental budget for the settlement of the payable.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer and the Acting MPDC and other concerned Department Heads to revisit relevant documents of the programs/projects funded by NGAs and implement them in order to achieve the purpose/s these funds.
Further, the Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts to determine the source agencies, nature and purpose/s of these fund transfers, date of the fund transfers, correct aging of payables in order to facilitate immediate implementation of the projects and return of any unused funds to the source agencies for the latter’s use for other priority PPAs.

The Audit Team recommended that Management judiciously and meticulously plan the PPAs to be included in the APP where all the required details such as the Name of Procurement Project; Procurement Management Office (PMO)/end-user/implementing unit; Method of Procurement; Schedule of identified procurement activities as reflected in the APP form approved by the GPPB; Source of funds; Indicative ABC; and other relevant descriptions of the project, if applicable, hence to have an accurate basis in the execution of the PAPs and in the conduct of proper procurement process in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9184.
Henceforth, the Audit Team recommended that Management enforce a centralized system on the procurement of supplies, materials and equipment for proper control on the acquisition and issuances thereof wherein the GSO will determine the items to be procured on a quarterly basis based on the Consolidated APP of the Municipality and require the BAC to conduct a public bidding for items not available in the PS-DBM, hence to avail more advantageous prices and to adhere with the policy of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency, and economy in the procurement process

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE:
ensure that identified programs/projects under the 20% DF are implemented as planned to fully attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the municipality;
require the concerned officials to review the feasibility of the unimplemented programs/projects and plan for the necessary realignment of these funds to a more viable priority development project; and
refrain from including in the development plan those projects that fall under the expenditures items that are not allowed to be charged from the 20% DF in adherence with Item 3.2.5 of JMC No. to achieve the purposes of the fund and avoid the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

The Audit Team recommended that Management refrain from utilizing the fund to programs/projects other than the authorized uses and prioritize the implementation of programs/projects which are aligned to the prescribed purposes/uses of the fund as provided under Section 2 of RA No. 7171 and Item 2.3 of the Local Budget Memorandum No. 89, contributory to the advancement of self-reliance of the tobacco farmers, to avoid the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer, in compliance with the directives of the issued COE, to cause the collection or settlement of the disallowances directly from the persons liable to avoid its referral to the General Counsel for appropriate action including referral to the Office of the Solicitor General and the Ombudsman.
Likewise, we recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant for the immediate settlement of the suspensions totaling P379,023,082.32 to avoid maturing of the same into disallowance.

observations.recommendation:
1.4.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to present fairly
the actual results of operations/transactions in their corresponding
records, hence the cashbook and the FS shows the correct and actual
balances of the accounts, in order to present a reliable information
to users.

1.5.5 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant
to review her records and trace back the foregoing discrepancies
between the accounts RPT/SET Receivables and the corresponding
Deferred RPT/SET versus the record or list of tax delinquencies kept
at the Treasury Office and draw the necessary adjusting entries in
order to reconcile the same and to reflect accurate balances of these
accounts in the FS.

1.5.6 Further, we recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal
Accountant to adhere with the correct recording of the advance
collection of RPT due in the succeeding year and the recognition of
income, to wit:

a) Upon collection:
Debit Cash in Treasury xxx
Discount on Advance Payment of RPT xxx
Credit Deferred RPT/SET xxx
# Receipt of RPT payment due for the succeeding year

b) Year when tax is due (following year):
Debit Deferred RPT/SET xxx
Credit RPT/SET Basic xxx
Discount on Advance Payment of RPT xxx
Due to LGUs xxx
# Adjusting entry to reverse the entry taken up
for the advance payment and recognition of income

1.6.6 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Budget Officer to make the necessary adjusting
journal entry to correct the erroneous recording of CIP-Road
Networks and CIP-Other Structures in the books of accounts and to
reflect the amount of P9,278,032.61 as savings, hence the correct
balances of the affected accounts would be fairly presented in the FS.
Likewise, we recommended that the savings be reappropriated to
other PPAs under the criteria of the RA 7171 fund, thus the funds
would be used fully for the benefits of the tobacco farmers.

1.7.4 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant
to prepare a correcting/adjusting journal entry to restate the FS of
the ensuing year, hence reflect the correct balances of the affected
accounts in the FS, to wit:

a) Debit Office Equipment 35,700.00
Disaster Response and Rescue 197,600.00
Equipment
Technical and Scientific 30,000.00
Equipment
Credit Prior Period 263,300.00
Adjustment
# To correct the erroneous recording of expenses
instead of PPE (voucher no. 510, 883 and 1208)

b) Debit Military, Police and Security 80,119.92
Equipment
ICT Equipment 207,450.00
Credit Office Equipment 287,569.92
# To correct the erroneous classification of PPE
(voucher no. 785,865 and 917)

1.9.3 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant
to observe the provisions of IPSAS I and Section 111 of PD 1445, thus
record in the appropriate CIP account only the billed cost of the
completed portion of any ongoing project and recognize the same in
the corresponding PPE account when fully completed, hence to
present fairly and reliably the FS of the Municipality.

2.7 We recommended that the LCE:
2.7.1 instruct the Inventory Committee to prioritize the conduct of a
thorough and complete physical inventory and reconcile the results
thereon with the accounting records to ascertain the accuracy,
completeness and existence of the reported PPE in the financial
statement;
2.7.2 instruct the Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee to
reclassify the properties costing P15,000.00 and below to semi-
expendable properties; and
2.7.3 instruct the Property Officer, the Head of Inventory Committee and
the Municipal Accountant to adhere with the guidelines and
procedures set forth under COA Circular 2020-06 dated January 31,
2020 for the conduct of physical count of properties; recognition of
PPE items found at station; disposition for non-existing/missing PPE
items; and for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, in
order to reflect more reliable and accurate balances of PPE accounts,
hence to convey precise information useful in decision making and
accountability for these properties.

3.8 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to locate
or trace the documents of recorded road networks and make the necessary
adjustments in the books recognizing the accumulated depreciation and
depreciation expenses to be recorded in the succeeding years to reflect the
correct book value of the road networks, hence to present fairly and reliably
the PPE, government equity, expenses and income accounts in the FS.

4.7 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to prepare
the necessary documents detailing the information relative to replaced parts
of road networks and other infrastructure assets and furnish a copy to the
Municipal Accountant as her basis in determining the appropriate carrying
amount of replaced parts of local road networks and other infrastructure
assets to be derecognized from the books of accounts in order to fairly and
reliably present these accounts in the FS.

5.9 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
5.9.1 Determine the unclaimed retention money for its possible payment
and coordinate with the Municipal Engineer for the issuance of

demand letters and ensure that the same is received by the concerned persons
and seek legal remedies so that the unliquidated cash advances of those
accoutable persons who are no longer in service will be settled the soonest

Bureau of Treasury or return the unutilized funds to the source agencies to
finally end any obligation created by these transfers and to fully liquidate these
accounts.

for purposes that have been completed or abandoned may be transferred by

available for appropriation to support local government programs and

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE create an Inventory Committee to conduct a periodic physical inventory of all the properties of the Municipality and ensure prompt submission of a duly and completely accomplished RPCPPE.  Likewise, we recommended that the LCE instruct the GSO Designate and the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the reconciliation of the RPCPPE and accounting records in order to present an accurate PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entry for the transfer of unexpended QRF-MOOE to fairly present the financial statement of the Municipality in compliance with Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to submit a certified list of taxpayers’ name and the amount due and collectible for the current year as the basis for the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher to record such receivables at the beginning of the year in order to ensure the correctness of the amount established to avoid the possibilities of under or over statement of receivable and the related accounts.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters and ensure that the same is received by the concern persons, seek legal remedies so that the unliquidated cash advances will be settled the soonest possible time. For the unliquidated cash advances of the deceased individuals, we recommended that LCE seek legal remedies for the institution of claim against their estates.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the designated HRMO to:
Maintain logbooks, Bundy Clocks or any other device where they can validate the daily attendance of regular employees and hired personnel under job order;
If the daily attendance will be monitored via cellphone application, the designated HRMO must still maintain logbook in addition to said application, for counter checking by the Municipal Accountant, especially in the event of power interruption, loss of cellphone signal and if other personnel or employees do not have smartphone capable of such application. Maintain printout copies from said application for verifications/validation of the time ins and outs.
Require submission of the DTRs, including Application for Leave and Travel Orders, for the regular employees and DTRs and accomplishments reports for personnel hired under job order within reasonable number of days after the end of the month.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to furnish the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year be given at the beginning of the year to the Office of the Auditor.  Also, provide a notice to the Office of the Auditor within ten days, after the award of infrastructure projects or before its start that the project signboard was already posted. If personal submission of said list or notice is not possible, the duly signed scanned copy of the list or notice could be submitted to the official email address of the Audit Team (r108agb@yahoo.com). This will enable the Auditor to furnish Management noted deficiencies, if any, for its immediate and appropriate action. More so, the constituents are also informed of such public concern, hence transparency for good governance is ensured. Likewise, it is encouraged that Management attach to the disbursement voucher pictures of the posted signboards taken at the project site.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the GSO-Designate or the official responsible in accepting deliveries to furnish a notice to the Office of the Auditor on the deliveries of procured items within 24 hours from the acceptance to comply with the requirement of COA Circular No. 96-010. If personal submission of said notice is not possible, the duly signed scanned copy of the notice could be submitted thru the official email address of the Audit Team (r108agb@yahoo.com) to enable the latter to conduct inspection of procured items to determine whether or not such deliveries conformed with the quantity, quality and specifications in the Purchase Order.  Thus, in case of any discrepancies, Management would be informed for its appropriate and immediate action. Likewise, it is encouraged that Management attach pictures of the items delivered to the Disbursement Voucher.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer to impose internal rules in the movement or flow of transactions, like the claiming of checks, in order to expedite submission of reports and documents to the Municipal Accountant for submission of the latter to the Auditor. Likewise, we recommended that the LCE remind the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts, in order to facilitate immediate review, evaluation and examination of consummated transactions, thus deficiencies, if any, could be communicated to Management for its immediate and appropriate action.

We recommended that the LCE instruct/oblige the Municipal Treasurer to assign only regular government employees to perform duties which permit or require the possession, custody or control of local government funds and which also require bonding of their fidelity. Likewise, we recommended that Management carefully examine if there is a need to open a new plantilla position to cater such function in order to comply with Sec. 101 of PD No. 1445, in order avoid possible misuse or loss of the government funds.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and MSWDO to submit the lacking documents and explain or correct the deficiencies noted to comply with the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 9 in order to ensure that the recipients are qualified beneficiaries and that the financial assistance were actually received by them.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to give preferential attention to the retrieval of records and determination of the intended purpose/s of the transferred funds for possibilities of implementing the projects/activities. In cases that the purposes were already accomplished, we recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer the immediate remittance of the balances to the Bureau of Treasury or return the unutilized funds to the source agencies to finally end any obligation created by these transfers and to the fully liquidate these accounts, hence, the amounts could be utilized by the source agencies/NGAs for other priority PPAs.
In addition, we recommended Management consider Section 2.9 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2 dated December 4, 2020 on the utilization of unexpended cash balances of public funds held in trust by LGUs for purposes that have been completed or abandoned may be transferred by the LGUs concerned to the respective general funds which could be made available for appropriation to support local government programs and projects in response to the Covid-19 pandemic.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to include the cost of implementation of CSHP as a separate pay item in the Program of Works pursuant to Items 5.2 and 5.3 of the DOLE Department Order No. 13, series of 1998 and Section III.A of the DPWH Department Order No. 56, series of 2005 to ensure protection and welfare of workers employed and the general public within and around the construction site.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 in order to substantiate the legality, validity and propriety of transactions and in order to avoid the issuance of NS. Likewise, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the immediate submission of the lacking supporting documents for audit purposes.

We recommended that the LCE remind the BAC to consider all the relevant activities indicated in Section 20 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, or any amendments thereto in the future, to ensure the readiness of the procurement and compliance with all the requirements before the issuance of the invitation to bid which are also determinants for successful procurement activities.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to invite observers from a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand and from an NGO in accordance with Section 13.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to enhance transparency of the procurement process.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC to compute their honoraria by grouping successfully completed procurement projects in a certain month and apply the proper computation as provided for in Section 15 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.  Also, require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payment would not exceed 25% of the basic monthly salary of the chairs and members, thus proper payment of honoraria.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC Chairman and the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents and see to it that all the requirements for emergency purchases pursuant to GPPB Circulars, Resolutions and the Government Procurement Reform Act or RA 9184 were followed and attached, to ensure transparency and accountability of procurement process.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold at least 1% of the contract amount for the retention money or require the posting of a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of the total contract price which shall only be released after the lapse of the warranty period or, in the case of Expendable Supplies, after consumption thereof to ensure that the supplier will correct manufacturing defects that maybe encountered within the warranty period.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer and the Acting MPDC and other concerned Department Heads to revisit relevant documents of the programs/projects funded by NGAs and implement them so that the purpose/s these funds were given will be achieved. We also recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts to determine the source agencies, nature and purpose/s of these fund transfers, and prepare aging of payables to determine how long these funds have been outstanding and facilitate liquidation and return of any unused funds to the source agencies. Thus, returned funds will be used by the source agencies to fund other priority PPAs.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the GAD Focal Person to maximize the utilization of the GAD Funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to fully attain the objective for which the funds were provided.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Planning and Development Council to ensure that priority programs/projects/activities under the 20% Development Fund are capital expenditure consistent with DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 in order not to deprived the constituents of the long-term benefits and real purpose of the said fund.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO to prepare and submit respectively, the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit to the Office of the Auditor to comply with COA Circular No. 2012-002 in order to facilitate timely review, verification and monitoring of the utilization of the said fund. For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we recommended the following:

That the Inventory Committee conduct the required annual physical count of each category of PPE as of December 31 of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the physical existence of the PPE and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pin point responsibility/accountability/liability for the missing PPEs. Further, the audit team should be invited to observe during the annual physical count;

That the Inventory Committee prepare the RPCPPE and submit it to the Auditor within the prescribed period; and

That the Municipal Accountant and the GSO/ Municipal Treasurer maintain/ update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and put property tags on all PPE items except land which are required under MNGAS for proper control, monitoring and easier identification of the PPEs.

taxpayers’ name and the amount due and collectible for the year as the basis for
the Municipal Accountant to draw JEV to record such receivable and that the
accounting treatment for Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax be
strictly followed in adherence to existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer promptly remit RPT and SET share of the Provincial Government of
Ilocos Sur in accordance with the above provisions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the transfer of
the unspent balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund
books by debiting the Cash in Bank account and crediting the Trust Liability –
LDRRMF account.

We recommended that management enhance its collecting strategy to
enable their employees to liquidate their cash advances. For those who are
already deceased, the management should seek legal remedies so that these
unliquidated cash advances be collected from their estate.

We recommended that the MDRRMO submit Monthly Report on the
Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to the COA
Auditor in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue the
preparation of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) in accordance
with COA Circular No. 96-011 and give the Auditor a copy. Meanwhile, we urge
her to itemize or determine, if possible, the unreconciled figures to establish the
correct accountabilities or balances of the accounts being maintained by the
municipality.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman
comply with the provisions set forth under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 12, 2009 regarding the submission of perfected contracts together with
its supporting documents to the Auditor.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit the GPB bearing
the approval of the PCW and corresponding Accomplishment Report within the
required time to facilitate immediate review of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require physical inventory
taking of all inventories reflected in the financial statements to ascertain their
accuracy and existence for a fair presentation.

We recommended that the municipal personnel concerned observe the
complete and proper accomplishment of Driver’s Trip Tickets pursuant to COA
Circular No. 77-61 to provide basis in determining the reasonableness of fuel
consumption of each municipal vehicle.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and employees in
charge in the preparation of the basic supporting documents for all claims
adhere to the provision set forth under Section 4 (6) of the P.D. 1445 to avoid
possible suspensions and disallowances in audit.

We recommended that management stop the practice of procuring goods
through reimbursement basis except for petty expenses and that payment be
made payable to the supplier or creditor in accordance with rules and
regulations.

We recommended the following measures/procedures to correct the
above deficiencies to ensure the validity and legality of the transactions:
a) That the Municipal Accountant see to it that all supporting
documents for all types of transactions are complete.
b) That the BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG Members of the
Municipality, at all times, be guided with the 2016 Revised IRR
of RA 9184 in the procurements of goods and services, and
infrastructure projects.

We recommended that:
a) Management revisit the programs funded by National
Agencies and properly implement them so that the desired
purpose will be achieved.
b) Management ensure that funds from National Agencies be
liquidated and unexpended balances should be returned to
source agencies to relieve them from any accountability.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the 20%
Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic
and environmental outcomes for the municipality and its intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board
oversee the effective implementation of the programs/projects/activities under
the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan such as the Sanitary Landfill
Development and adhere to the provisions of RA 9003 in order to ensure the
protection of public health and environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Council for the Protection of
Children see to it that the appropriation for the protection of children should be
equal to or more than one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment in
compliance with Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 and DILG Memorandum
Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 to ensure that all plans for children are
provided with funds and implemented.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all persons liable to
settle immediately the audit disallowances which had become final and
executory. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that
supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to
prevent the same from being suspended/disallowed in post-audit. Also,
management should settle audit suspensions and disallowances within the
reglementary period of 90 days and six months, respectively as prescribed under
the aforementioned rules and regulations.

For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we recommended the following:
a. Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the physical existence of the PPE and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pin point responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs.
b. Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.

observations.recommendation:
For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we recommended the following:
Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December of every year.  The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current  year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the physical existence of the PPE and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets.  Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pin point responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs.
Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.
We recommended that all concerned officials/personnel strictly adhere to COA Circular Nos. 2016-004 and 2015-008 dated September 30, 2016 and November 23, 2015, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books by debiting the Cash in Bank account and crediting the Trust Liability – LDRRMF account.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record correcting entries under the General Fund by debiting Trust- Liability – DRRM and crediting Prior Period Adjustment.

We recommended that management enhance its collecting strategy to enable their employees to liquidate their cash advances. For those who are already deceased, they should seek legal remedies so that these unliquidated cash advances be collected from their estate.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Revenue Officers follow the provisions of the New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs in managing the collections to safeguard the same from possible loss/misuse/misappropriations of government funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive establish procedures to identify what work or activities are exposed to hazardous areas and occupational risk in order to entitle the MSWDO the hazard allowance.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit the copies of the perfected contracts within five days after execution to facilitate timely evaluation of infrastructure projects, particularly on the basic supporting documents.

We recommended that the BAC require the submission from the winning bidders/contractors the duly approved Construction Safety and Health Program as forming part of the perfected contracts upon its submission for the contract review. Pending the approval by the DOLE of the program on the date of submission of perfected contracts for review, a certification that the program has been submitted for approval by the DOLE should be attached. Upon the final payment of these contracts, the Municipal Accountant should require said duly approved program. This is to ensure compliance with R.A. 9184 and issuance of the DOLE.
We recommended that management require the contractor to submit the materials test results to ensure the quality of the materials to be used for infrastructure projects.

We recommended that:
Management revisit the programs funded by National Agencies and to properly implement them so that the desired purpose will be achieved.
Management ensure that funds from National Agencies be liquidated and unexpended balances should be returned to source agencies to relieve them from any accountability.

We recommended that the municipality see to it that the programs/projects under 20% Development Fund are implemented for the benefit of the constituents. The municipal officials review their plan to determine if these programs and projects are no longer relevant for the attainment of development in the municipality so that the funds budgeted to these programs may be re-aligned to more useful and viable projects.
We recommended that the municipality revise its Market Code to meet the current situation in the locality in order to help the stall owners reduce their business expenses.

We recommended that the Municipality allocate a separate 1% from its IRA for their respective Local Council for the Protection of Children (LPCC) in order to have additional funds to support programs/projects that will benefit its constituents especially the children.

We recommended that management settle/appeal disallowances within six (6) months from receipt of the notice. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to prevent the same from being suspended/disallowed in post-audit. Moreover, the Municipality should also settle its remaining disallowances.

observations.recommendation:
For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we have recommended that the Management:
Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December of every year.  The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current  year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed assets and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets.  Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pin point responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs.
Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.
We have recommended that all concerned officials/personnel strictly adhere to COA Circular Nos. 2016-004 and 2015-008 dated September 30, 2016 and November 23, 2015, respectively.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit copies thereof to the Auditor for review and verification of the accuracy of the Cash in Bank accounts.
We have recommended that management employ diligence in causing the settlement of long outstanding unliquidated cash advances from persons responsible and liable.

We have recommended that the Municipality:
Grant cash advance other than traveling expenses to designated disbursing officer or the Municipal Treasurer only.
Require payees to sign the payroll as proof of payment.
Require authority from the claimant if payment is claimed by person other than the payee.
Require all officials and employees to submit their DTRs monthly.
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit their revenue and disbursement documents on time.
We have recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor countersign all checks drawn for the payment of expenses of the Legislative pursuant to the provision of Section 40 of the NGAS Manual.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of an Accomplishment Report for the disbursement of gasoline expenses in the rehabilitation of barangay roads and rechanneling of river to ascertain the validity, legality and completeness of the transactions.

For the proper implementation of the procurement process, we have recommended that the BAC Secretariat:
Post all Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed to the PhilGEPS website to attain transparency and efficiency in the procurement process.
Post all awards of small value procurement above P 50,000.00 in the PhilGEPS website.
Require contractors to submit results of materials testing performed by accredited agencies to ensure the quality of the materials used in the infrastructure projects.
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor not hire consultants whose duties and responsibilities are to be performed by regular employees to save government funds.

We have recommended that the Municipal School Board limit the utilization of the SEF to the programs and projects defined under RA 7160 in order to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail of under this fund. Also, some of the expenses should be charged to the schools since they have MOOE downloaded to them. With these, the utilization of SEF for its authorized purpose is ensured and maximized.
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be fully utilized for priority programs/projects and activities for the benefit of the constituents.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books by debiting the Cash in Bank account and crediting the Trust Liability – LDRRMF account.
We have recommended that the municipality charge disbursements to General Fund so as not to defeat the purpose of the LDRRMF and deprive the beneficiaries of the said fund.

We have recommended that management settle/appeal disallowances within six (6) months from receipt of the notice. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to prevent the same from being suspended/disallowed in post-audit.

We have recommended that the municipal officials and employees concerned adhere to the provisions of Section 124, Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for local government units and Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 to determine the validity, accuracy, and reliability of the property, plant and equipment owned by the municipality.  Further, the parcels of land owned by the municipality should be transferred and registered under the name of the LGU.
